lesson.title: Read the screen defender before you commit.
lesson.body_md:
## What is drop coverage?

On a ball-screen the defense has to choose: switch the matchup, blitz the ball, hedge and recover, or have the screen defender drop back and protect the rim. **Drop coverage** is the most common call at every level — the screen defender (the big) sits two or three steps below the level of the screen, belly forward, weight back, and gives the ball-handler one thing in exchange for taking away another: he gives up the pull-up, he protects the paint.

```takeaway
Drop coverage is an offer. The big is saying: you can shoot it, you can't drive past me. Read his chest before you commit.
```

## What visual cues reveal it?

1. **Chest line.** If his chest is below the level of the screen as you turn the corner, he is dropping. If his chest is at or above the level, he's hedging or stepping up.
2. **Foot angle.** Drop big's feet point back at his own basket — he is retreating, not stepping out.
3. **Distance to the screen.** Two-plus steps below the screen = drop. One step or less = hedge. Stepping over the screen = blitz or switch.

```tip
Find the chest first. Everything else is confirmation.
```

## Why the ball-handler has temporary space

The trade-off is geometric. A drop big cannot contest the pull-up without abandoning his real assignment, which is the rim. The on-ball defender is going under or trailing the screen — by definition behind you. For one beat, no one is in the pocket between the screen and the retreating big. **That beat is yours.**

```takeaway
Drop coverage = pocket pull-up window. It closes the moment help rotates or the big steps up.
```

## How to choose between pull-up / snake / attack / reset

- **Pull up** when the chest is clearly below the screen and on-ball is behind you. This is the default answer to drop coverage.
- **Snake** the dribble back to the middle when you misread the depth — keeps the possession and re-asks the question on the second turn.
- **Attack** the rim only if the big is stepping up or his angle changed mid-action (drop → hedge). Don't attack into a settled drop big — that is his job.
- **Reset** when the on-ball defender stays attached and the big is already stepping up, so the pocket never opens.

```mistake
Driving directly into the retreating big.
Fix: the space is in front of him, not past him. Read the chest, take the pocket.
```

## Self-check after the rep

```reveal
Q: Did I look at the screen defender's chest before I turned the corner?
A: His posture names the call. If you don't look, you're guessing.
```

```reveal
Q: Was the pocket open, and did I take it?
A: Drop coverage gives you one window. Hesitation closes it.
```

```reveal
Q: When did I miss — at the cue, or at the action?
A: Miss at the cue means re-watch the film. Miss at the action means take more reps.
```

## Try it

```quiz
Q: You come off a high ball-screen. The big's chest stays below the screen and his feet point back at the rim. What's the read?
- Drive at his chest into the paint ✗
- Pull up in the pocket ✓
- Reset to the wing ✗
Why: Drop coverage hands you the pull-up. The pocket is the offer.
```

```coach
The drop big is paid to give up the shot. Make him right by taking it.
```
